method: Sprout and boil the beans.
In a pan, sauté onion, tomato, and grated ginger.
Add salt, sugar, haldi, red chilli powder, coriander powder, and jeera powder.
Add the boiled beans and coriander leaves.
For the kat/masala: Grind garlic, ginger, misal masala, and dry coconut into a paste.
Sauté chopped onions and tomatoes, then add haldi, red chilli powder, and the ground masala.
Add salt, sugar, and water to get a watery texture.
For the potato mixture: Sauté jeera, mustard, haldi, onions, and green chillies, then add boiled chopped potatoes.
Assemble by layering beans, potato mixture, kat, onions, and coriander.
Serve with pav and lemon.
title: Roadside Kolhapuri Misal Pav
Avarekai Sagu
method: Boil the peas.
Grind coconut, cloves, cinnamon, ginger, pepper, chillies, elaichi peel, dhania, jeera, poppy seeds, 1 spoon of boiled avarekai, coriander, and nuts into a coarse paste.
In a pan, sauté jeera in oil, then add the masala paste and fry.
Add salt, sugar, boiled avarekai, and water.
Boil well and add lemon juice.
Roast garlic and slit tomatoes brushed with olive oil at 375°F for 35 mins.
Sauté onions in oil or butter, then add basil, roasted tomatoes, and garlic.
Add salt, pepper, and vegetable broth.
Boil well, then purée and strain.
Serve with cream, cheese, croutons, and garlic bread.
title: Roasted Tomato Basil Soup

method: Boil toor dal with 1.5 tomatoes and mash.
Dry roast and grind pepper, 2 tsp toor dal, dhania seeds, raw rice, and jeera.
Fry red chillies in a drop of oil and add to the ground mixture.
Boil the mashed dal with haldi, salt, and curry leaves.
Add a tadka of ghee, jeera, mustard, and hing.
Add chopped coriander, 1/2 chopped tomato, and the ground masala.
Add water and boil well.
title: Iyengar Kalyana Rasam
Dindigal Thalapakatti Egg Biryani
method: Fry boiled eggs with red chilli powder, garam masala, coriander powder, and salt.
Grind shallots coarsely.
Grind garlic, green chillies, and ginger into a paste.
Powder the biryani masala ingredients (elaichi, saunf, cloves, dagad phool, jeera, pepper, cinnamon, bay leaf).
Sauté ground shallots and the ginger-garlic-chilli paste in oil/ghee.
Add haldi, biryani masala, coriander powder, red chilli powder, salt, and curd.
Fry for 10 mins.
Add coriander, pudina, eggs, and rice.
Add 1.5 cups of water per cup of rice.
Cook on low flame for 10 mins or pressure cook for 2 whistles.
Finely chop the cucumbers, onion, tomato (deseeded), and parsley.
Toss them together with lemon juice, olive oil, salt, pepper, and chopped mint.
title: Israeli Salad

method: Soak quinoa, dals, methi, and poha for 6-8 hours.
Grind with a little water into a thick batter.
Add salt and ferment overnight.
Mix the chopped vegetables.
Spread a ladle of batter on a hot tawa, add the vegetable mix, flip, and cook until done.
Alternatively, sprinkle chutney pudi and veggies on the batter.
title: Quinoa Uttapam
Katori Chaat / Chaat Basket
method: Knead a dough with rava, maida, salt, oil, and water.
Rest for 20 mins.
Roll small portions, stick to an oiled katori, and deep fry until the dough separates.
Prepare moong mixture by sautéing onions, spices, and boiled moong dal.
Prepare potato mixture by sautéing boiled potatoes with spices.
Assemble by filling the katoris with mixtures, chutneys, onions, tomatoes, coriander, sev, and seasoned curd.
Fry jeera and pepper in ghee.
Fry the washed leaves in ghee until wilted.
Grind the leaves, spices, and coconut together.
Mix with watered-down curds.
Add a tadka of ghee, mustard, jeera, hing, and curry leaves.
title: Mixed Soppu Tambuli

method: Sauté garlic and spring onions in sesame oil.
Add chopped veggies and tofu, and fry until soft.
Add sauces, broth, salt, and pepper.
Boil for 5-10 mins.
Now mix in the corn flour to thicken the soup.
Let it boil for a few more minutes till thick.
Adjust the seasonings.
Garnish with crispy noodles, spring onion, and ginger before serving.
Serve hot.
title: Veg Manchow Soup

method: Fry the salt in a small pan without oil.
Grind the garlic and ginger without any water.
Add the warm salt and oil, which acts as a preservative.
Store it in an airtight bottle in the fridge.
title: Ginger Garlic Paste

method: Drain the urad dal very well and grind so you get a very dry paste.
Do not add water while grinding it.
Mix in the powdered rice and dal and place it in the fridge for at least 30 mins.
Now add in the salt, pepper, ginger, chillies, and curry leaves, and mix well.
Make the vadas in hot oil, dipping your fingers in water before every dip so the batter doesn't stick to your fingers.
Medu vadas are ready.
For dahi vadas or dahi bhalla, drop the vadas in a bowl of cold water and leave it for ten mins.
Squeeze out all the water and put them in the curd mixture.
Curd mixture: In a big bowl, beat the curds with a little sugar, salt, and chaat masala.
Mix well and add coriander on top.
Once you drop in the vadas, place it in the fridge for a few hours.
Add green bhel chutney, sweet chutney, and pomegranate seeds.
Sprinkle red chilli powder, jeera powder, and sev before serving.
title: Dahi Bhalla
Avocado And Bean Salad
method: In a bowl toss everything together.
Add the vinaigrette and cilantro on top.
Chill for 1 hour.
And serve.

title: Vegetarian Chilli

ingredients.quantity: 4 deskinned and chopped roughly or 1 can tomatoes with the liquid

method: In a soup pot add olive oil, bay leaf, onion, garlic, celery.
Fry.
Add bell peppers, carrots and corn.
Now add the powders, salt, chipotle pepper, black pepper powder, chopped tomatoes.
Add some fresh parsley.
Add the cooked beans.
Mash it a little with the back of your spoon.
Add vegetable broth or water and let it simmer at least for 30-45 mins until all the flavors are incorporated and the chili has thickened.
Add some lemon zest.
Serve hot with some sour cream, cut avocado and scallions.
In a skillet, add a little butter and cook the tortilla slightly.
Add 1 spoon of cheese, fold it, and cook till the cheese has melted.
Turn it to the other side and cook the tortilla until browned a bit.
Cut into triangles and serve with salsa and guacamole.
title: Cheese Quesadilla

method: In a pan add some butter, chopped garlic, and chopped shallots.
Fry well.
Add lemon juice, salt, pepper, and cilantro.
Mix in the rice, vegetable broth, or water and cook it on the stovetop.
You can use this as a filling in burritos or as a side with tacos and enchiladas.
title: Mexican Lemon And Cilantro Rice

method: In a pan, add olive oil, garlic, and onion, and fry well.
Add bell pepper, jalapeños, celery, carrots, and spinach, and fry a bit.
Now add cumin powder, paprika or chilli powder, and the cooked black beans.
Add a little lemon zest, water, salt, and pepper, and let it simmer for 15 mins till it's thick.
Garnish with cilantro and shallots.
Take 5 tortillas, add 2 spoons of the filling, and fold them.
In a baking pan, add 1 cup of enchilada sauce and arrange the filled tortillas.
Add the remaining sauce on top.
Garnish with cheese, salt, pepper, cilantro, and scallions.
Cover with aluminum foil.
Bake at 375°F for 20 mins covered, and 5 mins uncovered.
Serve with a dollop of sour cream and hot sauce on top.
title: Bean And Veggie Enchiladas

method: In a pan, add some olive oil.
To it, add chopped garlic and onion, and fry.
Add bell pepper and jalapeños, and fry a bit.
Now add the sweet potato and butternut squash, some salt, and a little water/vegetable broth, and simmer for 15 mins till cooked.
Add the powders and garnish with cilantro and scallions at the very end.
In a baking pan, pour 1/2-1 cup of the enchilada sauce at the bottom.
Layer each tortilla with a spoon of the filling, and top it with some Mexican cheese.
Fold the tortillas and layer 4-5 of them in the pan.
Pour the remaining enchilada sauce on top, add more cheese, and a little salt and pepper.
Cover it with an aluminum foil and bake at 375°F for 20 mins, then uncover it and bake it for 5 more mins.
Serve with sour cream, hot sauce, and some guacamole, chips, and salsa on the side.
title: Butternut Squash And Sweet Potato Enchiladas
Benne Biscuit / Nankhatai
method: Mix ghee and sugar.
Add the baking powder and flour slowly and keep mixing.
Add elaichi powder.
Make balls and bake at 300°F for 22-25 mins.
Let it cool on a cooling rack.
Mix the first three ingredients in a microwaveable bowl.
Microwave for 90 secs.
Now add the elaichi powder and saffron.
Mix without any lumps.
Microwave again for 1 minute and mix.
If it's watery, microwave for another minute.
Let it cool.
Roll the mixture into balls.
title: Kesar Peda

method: Heat oil in a wok.
Add garlic, onions, all the veggies, and salt, and stir-fry a bit.
Don't overcook it, as it has to be crunchy.
Add the vinegar, soy sauce, and the other sauces.
Turn off the stove.
Add pepper and salt.
Mix in the quinoa.
Serve hot.
title: Quinoa Fried Rice

method: In a wok, heat oil, add the veggies, and fry for a minute.
Add the sauces, chilli oil, and chilli vinegar, and mix well.
Add salt and pepper.
Now stir in the noodles and garnish with shallots.
Serve immediately.
title: Chinese Bhel
Fruit Trifle

ingredients.item: Fruits (Apple, kiwi, pomegranate, strawberries, raspberries, mango, banana)

method: Make custard according to instructions on the packet.
Dissolve 3 tbsp of custard in 1/4 cup of milk.
Boil 3 tbsp of milk, add sugar to it.
Add the dissolved custard powder mixing well on low flame making sure not to form lumps.
Let it set in the fridge.
Make Jello of your choice.
In a bowl add the cake slices at the bottom.
Add the jello on top.
Let it set.
Now add the custard layer, then fruits and whipped cream on top.
May repeat these layers if you please.
Let it set for at least 4 hours in the fridge.

title: Kattu Saru / Lemon Rasam
method: Boil toor dal with a little turmeric and mash well.
Take the mashed dal in a vessel.
Add the tadka (ghee, green chillies, mashed ginger, peppercorns, jeera, curry leaves and hing), 3 cups of water, little pepper powder if you like, coriander, salt.
Let it boil well.
Add lemon juice at the end.
Heat the cream, strain it, and add it to the shaved chocolate in a large mixing bowl.
Mix well.
Add the alcohol and vanilla essence, and mix well.
It shouldn’t be too watery, more like semi-solid in consistency.
Refrigerate it or leave it outside for 2 hours.
Take a melon baller or a 1 tbsp spoon to make balls.
Make sure to wear gloves to do this, or the chocolate will melt in your hands.
Roll them in cocoa powder, dried fruit powder, or powdered sugar.
Allow them to set for at least 1-2 hours.
title: Chocolate And Rum Truffles
Iyengar Bakery Khara(Spicy) Bread
method: Heat oil in a pan, add jeera, onion chopped, green chillies, red pepper flakes, pudina, coriander fry it.
Let it cool down.
Warm water for 30secs.
Add sugar, dissolve yeast in it.
Wait for ten mins until the yeast rises.
In a bowl mix flour, salt, oil, milk, curd and risen yeast.
Slowly add the fried onion mixture in the centre and fold it in and knead well.
Leave it in a dark place in the greased bowl until it rises/ doubles for approximately 1 hour.
Knead it again (deflate it) flatten it to the rectangle (for 5 mins) and fold well.
Take the bread pan grease it and flatten dough slightly to fill the pan and cover it with greased plastic wrap and leave it in a dark place for 1 hour.
Bake the bread at 350 degrees F for 35-40mins.
Remove from oven and cold it for 5 mins.
Invert and allow it to cool completely and cut.

title: Eggless Banana And Whole Wheat,Cranberries And Walnut Bread
method: Puree the bananas.
In a bowl mix the vanilla essence, milk, banana puree, oil/ butter.
Slowly add in the sugar, salt and mix.
Now add the flour in batches of 3, slowly incorporating it all in.
Throw in the walnuts, cranberries and mix.
Transfer it to a seasoned bread baking pan.
Add a few pine nuts on top.
Tap it on the kitchen counter a few times to remove the air bubbles.
Bake it in a preheated oven at 350d F for 45 mins or until a fork goes through and through without sticking.
Leave it alone on the kitchen counter for at least 10 mins.
Then overturn it and let it cool.

title: Tomato Pulav
method: In a pan add oil, ghee.
Add jeera, whole Garam masala and fry well.
Add onion, green chilli paste, ginger garlic paste and fry well until the raw smell goes.
Add the pureed tomato and let it boil well.
May add a little red chilli powder if needed.
Add salt and a pinch of sugar.
Let it boil well until the oil separates.
It should be a chutney like consistency.
Mix with rice and serve.

title: Pesto Bocconcini / Fresh Mozzarella, Cherry Tomato Caprese Salad
method: Toss in the olive oil with the pesto, salt and pepper.
Add the cheese and tomatoes to it and coat well.
Let it rest for 10 minutes.
Keep mounting a piece each of the marinated cheese, basil leaves and cherry tomatoes one after the other on a skewer.
Serve cold.

title: Fruit Chaat
method: Toss it all in together and serve cold.

title: Avocado Paapdi Chaat
method: Cut the avocado into small pieces.
Mix the powders, tomatoes, onions, carrots and lemon juice in a bowl.
Add 2 spoons of yogurt and beat it well.
Add a pinch of sugar, jeera powder, chaat masala, black salt and red chilli powder.
Arrange the paapdi on a plate as shown in the picture.
Sprinkle some chaat masala and red chilli powder in top.
Add a spoonful of the avocado mixture.
Add the green and the sweet chutneys on top.
Garnish with sev, coriander and curd mixture.
Serve immediately.

title: Vietnamese Veg Rolls With Peanut Butter Dip.

ingredients.quantity: marinated in 1 spoon of sriracha, 1 tsp soy sauce

method: Dip the rice paper for 30 seconds in warm water.
Spread it on a wooden slab and arrange the veggies, noodles, tofu, cilantro, mint.
Sprinkle some salt and pepper.
Squeeze lemon juice and fold.
Serve with peanut butter dip.
For peanut butter dip: Mix peanut butter, soy sauce, honey or sugar, hot sauce/sriracha, lemon juice and water.
Garnish with some sesame seeds if you like.

title: Roadside Masala Puri
method: Heat oil in a pan, add the ground masala and fry until the raw smell goes away.
Add the cooked peas with water and it boil for ten mins.
Add turmeric red chilli powder, salt, coriander powder.
Boil for at least ten mins.
Now assemble it.
Break the Puris or papdi, add the masala on top.
Then add green bhel chutney and sweet chutney.
Garnish with onion, tomato, carrots, coriander and sev.
Serve immediately.

title: Black Bean Soup
method: In a pan, add olive oil, to it add chopped onions, bay leaf, garlic, celery, carrots.
Fry.
Add paprika powder, salt, black pepper.
Fry.
Add the boiled beans and vegetable stock.
Mash it a bit.
Add the vegetable stock.
Discard the bay leaf.
Blend it in a hand blender making sure you leave some chunks.
Adjust the seasonings.
Serve with a dollop of sour cream and scallions.

title: Vegetable, Black Bean Taco

ingredients.quantity: Mexican blend or any cheddar cheese grated

method: Taco filling: In a pan add some olive oil, add chopped garlic, onion and fry well.
Add paprika powder, little oregano, salt, pepper, cumin powder and fry well.
Add the boiled black beans and corn.
Mix well.
Add cilantro at the end.
Cook the tortilla on both sides on a pan.
Steps to arrange the taco: Take one cooked tortilla, add 1.5 spoons of taco filling.
Garnish with salsa, hot sauce (optional), guacamole.
Garnish with chopped onion, jalapeños, coriander, lettuce and cheese at the very end.
Squeeze some lemon juice and enjoy it.

title: Paneer Tikka

ingredients.quantity: deseeded and chopped into big cubes

ingredients.item: Haldi, kasoori methi, salt, black salt, pepper, jeera powder, ginger garlic paste

method: Marinade: Mix 1 spoon red chilli powder, 1/2 cup beaten hung curd, Haldi, kasoori methi, salt, Shaan tikka masala-1 tsp, black salt, pepper, jeera powder and ginger garlic paste, besan mixture and ajwain.
Add lemon juice and Mix well.
Now add in the paneer, veggies and leave it at least for 5-6 hours in the refrigerator.
Now take a skewer and arrange the marinated paneer, bell peppers, tomato and onion and grill it in a tandoor or a gas grill.
Serve with mint chutney and a slice of lemon.

title: Vegetable Soup

ingredients.item: Vegetables: cabbage, potato, capsicum green beans

method: Fry the onion in butter.
Add all the vegetables to it.
Fry a bit.
Add salt, pepper and water.
Once they are boiled, blend it in the blender.
Shouldn’t be a puree but coarse.
Add more pepper and salt if needed.
Garnish with shallots, cabbage and capsicum.
Serve hot.

title: Hara Bhara Kabobs

ingredients.item: Dry powders- red chilli powder, Garam masala, little amchur, chaat masala

method: Squeeze out all the water from the Blanched spinach.
Mash the green peas, add potatoes, spinach to it.
Now add the dry powders, mint, coriander, salt and mix well.
Add the corn flour which helps to bind it all together.
Make patties and garnish with a cashew nut and fry in hot oil.
Serve with mint chutney.

title: Bhel Puri
method: In a bowl mix the onion, tomato, cucumber, potato along with salt, chutneys, little lemon juice, and chaat masala.
Break in some papdi or pain Puri Puris to it.
Add the mandakki and toss.
Now garnish with sev and coriander.
Serve immediately.

title: Red Bhel Chutney
method: Blend in chillies with the garlic and salt.
Add lemon juice at very end.
Mix.

title: Tomato Slice Chaat
method: Mix the onion, carrots with red chilli powder, chaat masala and pepper powder.
Slice the tomatoes.
Sprinkle salt, chaat masala, pepper on it.
Sprinkle some mandakki on it.
Add the vegetable mixture.
Add a little sweet and green bhel chutney on top.
Garnish with kadlekayi, sev and coriander and serve immediately.

title: Green Bhel Chutney
method: Blend it with water.
Add lime juice at the very end.
Store it in an airtight jar.

title: Palak, Corn Soup
method: In a pan add the palak, ginger piece and blanch it until soft.
Blend it.
Add butter/oil to it add a few jeera, onion and fry well.
Now add dry powders.
Fry until the raw smell goes.
Add the ground palak paste and some water.
Let it boil on simmer for at least five mins.
Add the boiled corn and boil it for a few more mins or until it’s a little thick.
Garnish with cream and serve hot.

title: Sprouted Moong Chaat
method: Toss in the sauce along with the veggies.
Add the moong beans, salt, chaat masala and mix well.
Garnish with sev and coriander.
Note: may add a crushed papdi to it if you please.

title: Kurkure Masala / Chaat
method: Toss in all the powders with the chutneys and the vegetables.
Add in the kadlekayi, kurkure and mix.
Add lemon juice if you like.
Garnish with coriander and sev.
Serve immediately.
Note: may add crushed nipattu to it as well.

title: Haldiram's Moong Dal Chaat
method: In a bowl mix in the dry powders with the chutneys, congress kadle kayi and the veggies.
Add moong dal and garnish with coriander and sev.
Serve immediately.

title: Hagalakayi Gojju
method: In pan add 1 tsp oil, add hagalakayi fry well until it wilts.
Add tamarind paste, jaggery, salt, haldi, water and boil well.
Now add the masala and add more water and let it simmer until thick.
Add tadka- mustard seeds, Chana dal, urad dal, curry leaves, hing at the very end.
Can be stored in the refrigerator for a few days in an airtight container.
Serve with hot rice, rotis.
Note: to remove bitterness from the hagalakayi soak it in the rice water, wash it well and use.

title: Didhir Mavinakayi Uppinakayi / Instant Cut Mango Pickle
method: In a bowl mix in the red chilli powder, salt, chopped mango pieces.
Powder mustard seeds along with the fried methi seeds and powder them.
Add it to the mango chilli mixture.
Let it rest for at least twenty to thirty minutes.
Make a tadka with 3 spoons oil, mustard seeds, haldi, hing.
Once cool add it to the mango mixture.
Mix well.
Cover and let it rest for at least a night.
Add it to a dry bottle and store it in an airtight jar in the refrigerator.
It can be kept for at least a month.
Serve it with curd rice or hot rice and ghee.

title: Kittle Hannu Sippe Gojju / Orange Peel Gojju

ingredients.quantity: around 10 cuties, washed and chopped small

method: Fry the chopped orange peel until it withers and turns a little dark making sure not to burn it.
Add tamarind paste, Bella, haldi, salt and a little water and let it cook for a few minutes.
Now add the ground masala.
Add more 1/2 cup of water and let it simmer for at least ten mins or until it absorbs all the flavor and becomes a little syrupy.
Add more water if it begins to burn.
Now add a tadka at the very end with mustard seeds, curry leaves, hing and a red chilli.
It's ready to serve.
Can store this in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to a month.

title: Masala Mandakki / Churmuri Type 1
method: In a big bowl mix the oil with all powders, and the veggies.
Add mandakki at the very end.
Serve it immediately.
Can add sev to enhance the taste but I don't always add it.
Make a cone with a banana leaf and serve it in it to get the authentic street feeling:).

title: Potato Curry For Masala Dosa
method: In a pan add oil, make tadka with mustard seeds, Chana dal, urad dal, curry leaves, red chilli, hing, curry leaves.
Add onions, green chilli, ginger and fry well.
Add haldi and salt.
Add the boiled and mashed potatoes and mix well.
Garnish with coriander.

title: Vegetable, Cheese Croquettes
method: In a pan add a spoon of oil.
Fry all the veggies a bit, until semi cooked.
Once cool add it to the mashed boiled potatoes, along with onion, ginger garlic paste, green chillies, dry powders and salt.
Now make a small patty.
Add the cheese the center and roll making sure you enclose the cheese completely.
Make a paste with corn flour and water.
Dip the croquettes in it and then roll in the bread crumbs.
Fry in oil.
Serve hot with sauce.

title: Guacamole
method: Scoop out the avocado and mash it well.
Mix the onion, tomato, green chilli with salt, pepper and lemon juice.
Add the scooped out avocado.
Mix all.
Add coriander.
Serve with nachos.

title: Cabbage, Onion Pakodas
method: Mix everything in a bowl.
Add the hot oil.
Add 3 spoons of water if needed or if the water is too thick.
Allow it to rest for ten mins.
Fry them in hot oil.
Serve with tomato ketchup and chai.

title: Goli Baje( Mangalore Bonda)
method: In a bowl mix the maida, rice flour, curd, salt, hing, chopped green chillies, curry leaves, ginger, soda.
May add 2 spoons of water if it’s too thick.
The batter has to be thick.
Add salt.
Mix well.
Let it rest at least for 10-12 hours.
Has to ferment well only then you get soft, fluffy and crisp bajjis.
Add chopped coriander.
Mix in chopped onions, cabbage if adding any just before frying.
Fry in oil.
Serve hot with tomato ketchup and chai.

title: Tzatziki Dip
method: Wash and grate the cucumber.
Chop it or grate it small and let it drain in a cheesecloth for at least 2 hours or until all the water drains out.
Adding salt helps drain out the water faster.
Blend the olive oil, garlic together.
Add it to the yogurt.
Add salt, pepper, chopped mint leaves.
Mix well.
Add the cucumber(water drained).
Add olives and refrigerate it.
Serve it with pita bread or chips.

title: Saame / Little Millet Pongal
method: In a pressure cooker add a little of ghee and fry the moong dal a bit.
Keep it aside.
Add more ghee, add jeera, curry leaves, hing, peppercorns, pepper powder, haldi, ginger, green chillies.
Add the washed millet and some salt.
Add 3-3.5 cups of water and pressure cook for three whistles.
Serve hot with chutney or gojju.

title: Brinjal Raitha / Mosaru Bajji
method: Rub some oil on the brinjal and make a few nicks and either burn it on the stove or grill it in the oven for 45 mins.
Once cool, chop into multiple pieces and remove the tip.
Grind the ingredients under masala.
Add it to the mashed brinjal.
Add chopped onions if you like.
Add the curd, salt.
Make a tadka and garnish with coriander.

title: Green Peas Masala
method: In a pan fry onion, tomato to be puréed.
Add saunf and Grind into a paste.
Now in a pan add ghee, add the remaining chopped onion.
Fry well.
Add the onion paste, fry well.
Add ginger garlic paste, tomato puree, dry powders.
Fry well until the oil separates.
Add kasoori methi, cashew or almond paste and mix.
Add the green peas and a little water.
Add milk and cook for a few mins.
Garnish with coriander.

title: Sabut Masoor Dal
method: In a pan add ghee, add the whole Garam masala, chopped garlic, chopped onion, slit green chillies.
Fry well.
Add tomato, add dry powders.
Fry well until the oil separates.
Add the boiled sabut Masoor, salt, water and kasoori methi.
Let it boil for a few mins.
Add the grated ginger on top.
Garnish with coriander leaves and cream at the end.
Serve it hot rotis or jeera rice.

title: Navane( Foxtail Millet) Upma

ingredients.item: Peanuts, mustard seeds, Chana dal, urad dal, curry leaves, hing

method: In a pan boil 2.5 cups of water.
Add navane and cook until done.
Fluff it with a fork.
In a pan add oil, make tadka with peanuts, mustard seeds, Chana dal, urad dal, curry leaves, hing.
Add onions, green chillies, ginger and the other veggies if adding any and Fry well.
Add tomato, haldi.
Fry.
Add the lemon juice, coriander, salt.
Mix the cooked navane.
Serve hot.

title: Masala Oats
method: In a pan add a spoon of ghee/ oil.
Add a little jeera.
To it add onions, green chillies.
Fry well.
Add tomatoes, dry powders, salt, haldi.
Add the cooked veggies and mix.
Now add the oats, water and let it cook well.
Add more water and it have to be soft and mushy.
Add coriander and serve hot.

title: Churmuri Type 2

ingredients.item: Red chilli powder, Garam masala/ rasam powder, chaat masala, salt

method: In a bowl add the oil, powders, salt and mix.
Now add the vegetables, peanuts and mix.
Crush in the nipattu, and chips and mixture.
Add the mandakki at the very end.
Garnish with coriander.
Serve immediately.

title: Herekayi / Potato Bonda
method: Mix besan, rice flour, red chilli powder, salt, haldi.
Add hot oil carefully.
Slowly add a little water and make a thin watery paste.
Heat oil and slowly dip the potato/ herekayi coated in batter.
Once it’s done in both sides drain it on a paper towel.
Serve hot with some tomato ketchup and chai.

title: Mandakki Mixture

ingredients.item: Mustard seeds, curry leaves, red chillies, hing

method: Fry the mandakki a bit so it stays crisp.
Set it aside.
In a pan add oil to it add peanuts, mustard seeds, hing, curry leaves, red chillies, cashew nuts.
Add red chilli powder, sugar and salt.
Turn off the stove and mix in the mandakki.
Store it in an airtight jar.

title: Nimbe Sippe / Lemon Peel Chutney.

ingredients.item: Hingu, mustard seeds (1 spoon), menthya (2 spoon), red chillies (10), tamarind paste (1/4 spoon), bella

method: Store squeezed lemon peels in a bottle with salt in the refrigerator until ripe.
Fry all masala ingredients.
Add haldi and salt.
Pressure cook the lemon peels with minimal water.
Once cool, grind the masala and boiled peels together.
Mix well and store in an airtight jar in the refrigerator.

title: Jeera, Dhania Kashaya.
method: Fry the jeera and dhania seeds.
Add this to two cups of water and boil it for at least 10 mins or until the water reduces in quantity.
Consume it hot.

title: Spicy Shavige

ingredients.item: Oil, peanuts, mustard seeds, Chana and urad dal, curry leaves, hing

method: Boil the shavige according to instructions and add cold water to stop cooking.
In a pan, make tadka with oil and specified ingredients.
Add capsicum, green chillies, ginger and fry.
Add boiled or frozen green peas, salt, and haldi.
Turn off the stove, add lemon juice, mix with shavige, and garnish with coriander.

title: Brocolli, Raisins And Mayo Salad
method: Toss all ingredients together in a bowl.
Garnish with cheddar cheese.
(Note: Sugar is optional but omitted in this version).

title: Mint And Yogurt Chutney For Kebabs

ingredients.item: Amchur, jeera powder, chaat masala, salt, black salt

method: Grind all ingredients together in a mixer.
Mix well and serve cold with kebabs.

title: Potato Kebabs

ingredients.item: Dry powders (amchur, jeera powder, red chilli powder, Garam masala, chaat masala)

method: In a bowl, mash the boiled potatoes and add onions, green chillies, ginger garlic paste, coriander, and mint.
Add the dry powders and mix well.
Dip the bread slices in water, squeeze out the excess water, and mix into the potato mixture.
Add the rice flour and mix everything together.
Form small balls and pat them into patties.
Fry in hot oil until golden and drain on a tissue.
Serve hot with mint chutney or tomato sauce.

title: Ghugni Chaat

ingredients.item: White peas (ghugni), soaked and pressure cooked
Dry powders (red chilli powder, Garam masala, haldi, dhania powder, jeera powder)
Toppings (jeera powder, chaat masala, black salt, chopped onion, green chilli, tomato, coriander, chutneys, yogurt, and sev)

method: In a pan, heat oil and add jeera, bay leaf, and onion.
Fry well.
Add ginger garlic paste and fry.
Add tomato and the dry powders; fry well.
Add the cooked ghugni and mash it slightly.
Add salt and water, then boil until thick.
Garnish with coriander.
To serve, place ghugni in a bowl and top with the listed toppings.
Serve immediately.

title: Jhal Muri

ingredients.item: Dry powders (red chilli powder, jeera powder, Garam masala powder, chaat masala, black salt)

method: Mix all the dry powders with the mustard oil.
Add the chopped vegetables, peanuts, and boiled chana, then toss to combine.
Add the puffed rice at the very end to keep it crunchy.
Garnish with sev, mixture, coconut, coriander, and a squeeze of lemon juice.

title: Kaju Masala

ingredients.item: Cashews (1 cup whole for frying + 1/4 cup for paste)
Whole Garam masala (bay leaf, elaichi, cloves, cinnamon, saunf, jeera)

method: Fry 1 cup of cashews in ghee/butter until lightly browned; set aside.
Fry 3/4 of the onions, let cool, and grind into a fine paste.
Puree the remaining 1/4 cup of raw cashews.
In a pan with ghee/butter, add whole Garam masala and the remaining chopped onions.
Fry well.
Add the onion paste and ginger garlic paste; fry until the raw smell disappears.
Add tomato puree, dry powders, and haldi.
Fry well.
Stir in the cashew paste, fried cashews, capsicum, salt, sugar, and a little water.
Let it boil.
Finish by adding whipped cream, kasoori methi, and coriander.
Turn off the stove and serve with bread or rice.

title: Kachumber Salad

ingredients.item: Cucumber, carrots, red onion, and tomato, finely chopped
Black pepper, lemon juice, and salt

method: Toss all the chopped vegetables together in a bowl.
Add the dressing (pepper, lemon juice, salt) and mix well.
Garnish with coriander and serve cold.

title: Rainbow Salad
method: In a salad bowl, toss all the beans and vegetables together.
Mix the dressing ingredients in a separate bowl.
Add the dressing to the salad at the very end and toss well.
Garnish with fresh coriander.

title: Masoor Dal Khichdi

ingredients.item: Vegetables (Potato, peas, carrots, green beans, cauliflower)

method: In a pressure cooker, add ghee and the whole Garam masala.
Add onions and slit green chilli; fry well.
Add ginger garlic paste and fry.
Add tomatoes and dry powders; fry well.
Add the vegetables and salt, then toss briefly.
Add the dal and rice, frying for about 2 minutes.
Add approximately 3 cups of water and cook for 3 whistles.
Garnish with coriander and cut mint leaves.
Serve hot with raitha, papad, and pickle.

title: Gujarati Khichdi
method: In a pressure cooker, heat ghee and add hing, curry leaves, whole Garam masala, and jeera.
Fry briefly.
Add the moong dal and fry for a few minutes to remove the raw smell.
Add green chilli paste, haldi, rice, and salt; mix well.
Add coriander, lemon juice, and about 3 cups of water.
Pressure cook for at least 3 whistles.
Serve with papad, khadi, and pickle.

title: Dantina Soppu And Avarekai Huli Sambhar
method: In a pan, boil the chopped amaranth leaves.
Add the boiled avarekai, tamarind paste, haldi, and jaggery.
Stir in the ground masala and let the mixture boil.
Add tadka at the very end.
Serve with rotis or hot rice with ghee and papad.

title: Tomato Rasam (No Dal Rasam)
method: Boil the tomatoes in a deep vessel.
Once boiled, puree or blend them.
Add water, rasam powder, haldi, tamarind paste, jaggery, and salt.
Let it boil well.
Prepare the tadka with ghee and the specified spices, then add to the rasam.
Garnish with coriander.

title: Avarekai / Flat Beans Husli
method: Heat oil in a pan and prepare the tadka with mustard, dals, curry leaves, and hing.
Add turmeric and the ground masala; fry well.
Add the boiled beans, sugar, and salt; mix thoroughly.
Add lemon juice at the end and garnish with coriander.

title: Paan Shots
method: Remove the stems from the betel leaves and tear them into pieces.
Blend the leaves with all other ingredients until smooth.
Garnish with pista or badam powder if desired.
Serve cold in shot glasses as a post-meal treat.

title: Couscous Chitranna
method: Boil water and add couscous.
Cover, turn off the stove, and let it sit for 5 minutes.
Fluff with a fork.
In a pan, make the tadka with oil and the specified ingredients.
Add onion, green chillies, ginger, capsicum, and haldi; fry well.
Add carrots and peas with a few spoons of water; cook until done.
Turn off the stove, add salt, lemon juice, and the fluffed couscous.
Mix well and garnish with coriander.

title: Spinach And Asparagus Soup
method: Grill asparagus in the oven (375°F for 20-25 mins) or cook in a pan with olive oil until done.
In a pan, blanch the spinach, walnuts, and ginger in olive oil.
Once cool, add cumin powder, pepper, salt, and broth.
Boil for 5 minutes.
Blend with an immersion blender to a puree consistency.
Simmer briefly and serve hot with a dollop of butter or cream.

title: Tomato And Toor Dal Rasam
method: Blend the boiled dal and tomatoes together.
Add water, tamarind juice, jaggery, and salt; bring to a boil.
Once boiling, add the rasam powder.
Prepare the tadka with ghee and spices, then add to the rasam.
Garnish with coriander and serve with hot rice and ghee.

title: 7 Cup Burfi
method: In a pan add 2 spoons ghee and roast the besan a bit.
Once the raw smell goes, add the sugar, milk, coconut and fry a bit.
Making sure it doesn’t burn.
Add the ghee.
Keep stirring until it separates into one ball like consistency and doesn’t stick to the pan anymore.
Spread it on a ghee snared plate and cut it into pieces.

title: Carrot Payasa / Kheer
method: Roughly chop the carrots, blanched almonds and boil it in 2 spoons of milk until soft.
Once cool puree it.
Boil milk on simmer while making sure it doesn’t burn.
Add the sugar, elaichi powder, puréed carrots and saffron and keep boiling until thick kheer like consistency.
Garnish with dry fruits and serve it cold.

title: Vegetable Kurma

ingredients.item: Veggies (potato, carrot, peas, capsicum, cauliflower, green beans)

method: In a pan add a little oil/ butter/ ghee+ oil.
Add a little jeera, 1 bay leaf, 2 cloves, 1 elaichi.
Now add chopped onion and fry well.
Add the onion paste.
Fry until the raw smell goes.
Add the ginger garlic paste, fry.
Add tomato puree, poppu paste and all the dry powders and fry well until the oil separates.
Now add the almond paste.
Add all the veggies, add some salt, water and let it simmer until the veggies are cooked well and the gravy thickens.
Add kasoori methi and mix.
Garnish with coriander and cream.

title: Dry Fruit Burfi
method: Grind the figs and dates to a nice paste without adding any water or milk.
Coarsely crush other nuts making sure not to powder them.
In a pan add ghee, to it add the fig and date paste.
Fry it until all the ghee gets absorbed.
Now add the elaichi and nutmeg powder.
Mix.
Turn off the stove and add the other nuts powdered.
Mix them well.
Put it into a tray and pat them lightly with wet fingers or a bowl lightly smeared with ghee.
Cut it when a greased knife.
Refrigerate it for an hour at least.

title: Peanut Chutney
method: In a pan fry the peanuts in a tsp of oil along with the coconut, ginger, green chillies, curly leaves.
Once cool grind it with, tamarind paste, jaggery, a little warm water.
Add the tadka at the very end.

title: Green Apple Chutney
method: Grind the Apple, green chillies, ginger, tamarind paste, coriander, coconut with the sugar.
Add a little water.
Make a tadka and add it at the very end.

title: Kattu Saru / Lemon Rasam
method: In a vessel add the boiled and mashed dal and tomato or make the dal water by straining the cooked dal.
Add at least 2 cups of water, haldi and salt.
Make the tadka with ghee, few mustard seeds, jeera, slur green chillies.
Add crushed ginger and burn it a bit.
Add pepper a little ground, curry leaves, hing.
Add this to the dal water.
Let it simmer for at least 20 minutes.
Now add coriander and lemon juice.

title: Halu Menasina Saru / Pepper, Milk, Rasam
method: Fry the pepper, jeera, raw rice, urad dal, curry leaves, dry coconut in ghee and dry powder them.
Boil the powder in water, add a piece of jaggery, haldi, salt.
Once it reduces turn off the stove.
If you’re adding milk strain it and add milk.
Add ghee tadka with mustard seeds, jeera, curry leaves, hingu.

title: Dalimbre Sippe / Pomegranate Peel Tambuli
method: Fry the jeera, black pepper, pomegranate peel a bit.
Once cool grind it in a mixer with coconut, salt and some curd into a smooth paste.
Add more curd and tadka with few mustard seeds, jeera, hing and curry leaves at the very end.
Serve with hot rice.

title: Carrot, Cucumber Kosambari
method: Once the moong dal is soft, toss it in a salad bowl with the grated carrots, tadka.
Season it with salt, pepper, lime juice.
Garnish with coriander and coconut.
Serve cold.

title: Kayi Sasive Awalakki / Poha

ingredients.item: oil, peanuts, mustard seeds, Chana and urad dal, curry leaves and hing

method: Grind the masala (mustard, coconut, tamarind, red chillies) making sure it’s not too fine.
Don’t add any water.
Let it be a little coarse.
In a pan add oil, mustard seeds, dals, peanuts, curry leaves, hing.
Fry well, once done, add the masala and fry a little.
Add the jaggery and salt.
Bring to a boil.
Now add the poha and mix well.
Garnish with coriander.
Serve with curd.

title: Awalakki / Poha

ingredients.item: onions, tomato, capsicum, green peas and carrots
oil, mustard seeds, Chana and urad dal, curry leaves, green chillies, ginger, pinch of hing
coriander, lemon juice and little coconut

method: In a pan make the tadka, to it add chopped onions and fry well.
Add the tomato and other veggies and let it cook a bit.
Add a little sugar and salt.
Add the washed and drained poha, mix.
Sprinkle a few drops of water and cover and cook until soft.
Garnish with coriander, lemon juice and a little coconut (optional).
Serve hot with curd/ sev/ chips.

title: Jeera Rice And Dal
method: Jeera rice: In a pan add oil, jeera, hing, onion, green chillies.
Fry well.
Add the washed rice, salt and around 1.5 cups of hot water.
Once done fluff it with a fork.
Dal: In a vessel add oil, make tadka with mustard and jeera.
Add onion, green chillies and fry.
Add the garlic chopped and fry.
Now add tomatoes, fry powders and salt.
Add the dal and add enough water for it to cook well.
Keep on simmer and let it boil well until soft and mushy.
Garnish with coriander and julienned ginger.
Serve hot with jeera rice or rotis.

title: Dosa Batter
method: Soak the ingredients for 4-5 hours.
Grind the dals, poha and methi seeds to a fine paste with very little water.
Slowly add the rice, a little at a time and grind it.
Let it ferment for at least 10 hours or overnight.
Once fermented add salt and mix and refrigerate.

title: Basundi / Rabdi

ingredients.quantity: few strands soaked in two spoons of warm milk

method: In a heavy bottomed pan add the milk and sugar.
Add a little saffron, half elaichi powder, almond powder, and few cashews.
Keep stirring on low flame.
Add the condensed milk and continue stirring until it thickens completely.
Adjust the sugar and turn off the stove once thick.
Garnish with dry fruits, saffron and the left over elaichi powder.
Refrigerate and serve cold.

title: Idli Batter Recipe
method: Soak urad dal, methi seeds, and poha for 4-6 hours.
Grind them with very little water.
Soak idli rava for 20-30 mins.
Add it to the ground dal mixture and run it on pulse to mix.
Allow it to ferment.
Add 1 spoon of oil and salt.
Refrigerate.

title: Gongura Poppu / Dal

ingredients.item: ghee, mustard seeds, little jeera, curry leaves, hing, red chillies

method: In a pressure cooker add toor dal, gongura leaves, chopped tomato, green chillies, and haldi.
After three whistles, once cool, add a little water and salt if needed.
Fry chopped onion, garlic, red chilli powder, and coriander powder.
Add water if needed and bring to a boil.
Add tadka with ghee, mustard seeds, jeera, curry leaves, hing, and red chillies.
Serve with rotis or hot rice and ghee.

title: Quinoa Dosa Batter
method: Soak quinoa, rice, urad dal, and methi for at least 4-5 hours.
Soak poha separately and add at the very end.
Grind the urad dal and methi seeds with very little water.
Add rice and quinoa a little at a time and grind to a smooth paste.
Add poha and grind.
Allow it to ferment overnight or until it doubles, then add salt and refrigerate.

title: Bendekayi / Okra Gojju
method: Fry urad dal, dhania seeds, white til, and methi seeds.
Fry red chillies in a spoon of oil.
Grind all this when cool with coconut.
In a pan, add little oil, add the cut okra and fry well until it turns a little black.
Add tamarind paste, jaggery and water and let it boil.
Add the masala, salt and more water and let it boil until it thickens.
Add tadka and serve with rice/rotis.

title: Corn Bhel
method: In a bowl mix all the chutneys, chopped onions, tomatoes, and grated carrots.
Add chaat masala and salt.
Add the boiled corn.
Throw in the papdis.
Garnish with coriander, sev and a few congress kadlekayi.

title: Pudina Pulav / Mint Pulav

ingredients.item: Whole Garam masala (jeera, cloves, cinnamon, bay leaf, elaichi, star anise, black pepper)

method: Grind pudina, green chillies, tomato.
In a pan add oil and ghee add jeera, pepper, bay leaf, cloves, elaichi, cinnamon, cashews, onions, ginger garlic paste.
Add the ground masala, salt, let it boil on simmer until the oil separates.
Mix with rice.
Serve hot with raitha and chips.

title: Chocolate Fudge
method: In a pan fry the flour until the raw smell disappears.
Add milk and mix well making sure there are no lumps.
Add sugar and keep it on summer.
Add the cocoa powder and keep stirring.
When half done add a little butter.
Keep stirring continuously or it may stick to the bottom.
Add more butter.
When it becomes a thick chocolate consistency and leaves edges, pour it on a buttered plate and cut with a knife.
Chill it and serve cold.
Can add dry fruits to it.
It roughly takes 1.5-2 hours to completely thicken.

title: Mooli / Raddish Raitha
method: Grate the mooli, add onion, chopped green chillies.
Make a tadka with oil, mustard seeds, Chana dal, urad dal, hing, curry leaves.
Add coriander, salt and curd.
Serve with rice or rotis.

title: Beetroot Raitha
method: Boil the beets and once cool, chop them into small pieces.
Grind coconut with green chillies.
Add it to the beets.
Add tadka made with oil, Chana dal, urad dal, hing and curry leaves.
Add curd.
Garnish with coriander leaves.
Serve with hot rice or rotis.

title: Ridge-Gourd / Herekayi Peel Chutney
method: Fry the herekayi peel.
Fry the urad dal, coconut, red chillies.
Once cool blend it with tamarind paste and Bella.
Add the tadka.
Serve with dosas, Idlis or hot rice.

title: Onion Chutney

ingredients.item: Salt, piece of jaggery or sugar

method: Take 1 spoon of oil in a pan.
Fry urad dal- making sure not to burn it.
Add onion, red chillies, coconut and fry a bit.
Once cool grind it with the coconut, tamarind and jaggery.
Add the tadka at the end.
Serve with dosas, idlis or with hot rice.

title: Togribele / Toor Dal Chutney
method: Shallow fry the toor dal.
Fry the dry coconut, red chillies.
Once cool grind it a mixer with some salt and little water.
Make tadka with oil, mustard seeds, chana dal and urad dal, hing and curry leaves.
Garnish with raw onions( optional).
Serve with hot rice or dosas or idlis.

title: Shunti / Ginger Tambuli

ingredients.item: Green chilli (burn a little bit) or red chillies

method: In a pan add ghee, fry shunti, jeera, green chilli, coconut.
Grind once cool with curd and salt.
Make a tadka with ghee, mustard seeds, little jeera, hing and curry leaves.
Serve with hot rice.

title: Methi Pulav

ingredients.item: Whole spices (cloves, cinnamon, bay leaf, elaichis, star anise, black pepper)

method: In a pan add ghee and oil, to it add 1/2 spoon jeera, little saunf.
Add the whole spices, once done add the onions and fry well until golden.
Add ginger garlic paste, fry.
Add green chilli paste, cut tomatoes, fry.
Add methi chopped, frozen green peas and chopped potatoes if you want, salt according to taste.
Fry a bit.
Add the rice and fry for a min.
Add water and transfer it to a rice cooker.
Garnish with cashews and coatinafer leaves.
Enjoy it with raitha and chips.

title: Cold Pasta Salad

ingredients.item: Veggies (brocolli, bell peppers, boiled peas, carrots, cucumbers, cherry tomatoes)

method: Toss the veggies with vinegar, sugar, salt, oregano, pepper.
Add the pasta and toss it well.
Can be served cold.

title: Brocolli Cheddar Soup
method: In a pan add the butter, add chopped onions, fry.
Add maida and fry until the raw smell goes.
Add the milk/ half and half stir continuously making sure there are no lumps.
Fry celery and carrots a bit.
Add half the quantity of the cheese.
Now add brocolli and vegetable stock, cook for a few mins.
Throw in the salt and pepper.
Once half done, blend it with a hand blender.
Add the cheese and wait until it melts.
Adjust the seasonings.
Serve hot with a slice of garlic bread.

title: Lentil Soup

ingredients.item: Vegetables (carrots, cauliflower, green and red bell peppers)

method: In a pan add olive oil, to it add onions, bay leaf, garlic, fry well.
Add pepper, cumin powder, salt.
Add vegetables, parsley and fry a bit.
Now add lentils and fry for 5 mins.
Add vegetable stock and let it boil.
Once the lentils are done blend it with a hand blender after discarding the bay leaf.
Adjust the seasonings and the thickness by adding more stock if needed.
Serve hot with some sour cream and scallions.

title: Minestrone Soup
method: In a soup pot or a deep container add olive oil, onions, garlic.
Fry well.
Add the celery chopped and fry a bit.
Now add the finely chopped veggies, salt, pepper, parsley chopped, lemon zest.
Add the tomato puréed.
Throw in the pasta.
Now add the vegetable stock.
When half done add the beans and let it boil well for at least 20 mins.
Serve hot with some garlic bread.

title: Bisibelebath

ingredients.item: Tadka (ghee, mustard seeds, curry leaves, hing, red chillies, cashewnuts)

method: Cook the dal in the pressure cooker with a handful of peanuts.
Add the tomato, veggies to a big vessel.
Once half done add the rice, add 2 cups of water, some tamarind paste, small piece of jaggery, little haldi.
Let it boil.
Add the dal and the bisibelebath powder.
Adjust the seasonings.
Add a little dry coconut on top.
This has to be a stew like consistency so add more water if needed.
Make the tadka and add it at the very end.
Serve hot with ghee, papad and raitha.

title: Basale / Malabar Spinach Tambuli

ingredients.item: Salt, ghee for tadka and frying

method: In a spoon of ghee lightly fry the jeera, pepper and the leaves until they almost wilt.
Once cool grind it with the coconut, salt and 1 spoon of curd.
Add more curd and tadka with jeera, curry leaves and hing if you please.
Serve with rice.

title: Samosa Chaat / Ragda Samosa

ingredients.item: Chaat masala, chilli powder, black salt, jeera powder
Yogurt mixture (yogurt, red chilli powder, chaat masala, sugar)
Garam masala, red chilli powder, jeera powder

method: In a pan add oil, jeera.
Once it pops, add onion, fry.
Add ginger garlic paste.
Fry well.
Add chopped tomatoes, dry powders.
Fry well until the oil separates.
Add the boiled peas and mash it a little with the back of the spoon.
Add water and let it boil well.
Layering: Add samosa, on top add the green and sweet chutney.
Add the ragda on top, add chopped tomatoes and onions, sprinkle the dry powders, more green and sweet chutney.
Garnish with sev and coriander, one spoon of the yogurt mixture.

title: Peas Pulav

ingredients.item: Whole spices (cloves, cinnamon, elachi, star anise, bay leaf, peppercorns)

method: In a pan add oil/ ghee or both.
To it add a little jeera, all the whole spices, cashews, onions chopped, ginger garlic, green chilli paste.
Fry well at least for 5 mins.
Add green peas.
Fry for a minute.
Add washed rice and boiled water and salt.
Garnish with coriander and pudina.
Cover the pressure cooker and cook for 1 whistle only.
Once cooked fluff it with a fork.
Serve hot with raitha and chips.

title: Doddapatre / Cuban Oregano Tambuli
method: In a pan add some ghee, to it add pepper, jeera, doddapatre leaves and fry.
Once cool grind it with 1 tsp curd until smooth.
Add more curd and salt.
Can add a tadka with ghee, jeera, pinch of hing and curry leaves.
It helps cure an upset tummy or even sore throats.

title: Masala Puri
method: Grind mint, coriander and little boiled peas.
In a pan add oil, jeera and once it pops add chopped onion, ginger garlic paste and fry until the raw smell goes.
Add the tomato puree and the dry powders and fry well.
Add the ground pudina coriander mixture.
Add the peas, salt, tamarind paste, pinch of sugar, haldi.
Add water and let it simmer until thick.
Crush the puri’s, add the masala/ ragda, garnish with chopped onion, tomato, coriander, sev and a pinch of chaat masala.

title: Cucumber Raitha

ingredients.item: Tadka (oil, mustard seeds, chenna dal, urad dal, hing, curry leaves)

method: Grind coconut, coriander leaves, green chillies and salt to a chutney like consistency adding a little warm water.
Add it to the cucumber.
Add salt, curd and the tadka at the end.
Goes well with pulavs, bisibelebath or with plain rice.

title: Vegetable & Barley Soup
method: In a soup pan add olive oil, add chopped onion and garlic, bay leaf, fry well.
Add the tomatoes and fry.
Add the veggies, spinach, chopped parsley.
Add beans, peas, corn.
Now add the vegetable stock, pepper, salt, lemon zest.
Now add the barley or until cooked and let it simmer for next 20 mins.
Discard the bay leaf.
Serve hot with a slice of garlic bread.

title: Rasam With Dal

ingredients.item: Tadka (ghee, mustard seeds, jeera, curry leaves, hing)

method: In the pressure cooker boil the dal and tomato.
Mash well.
I blend it with a hand blender.
Add water.
To it add tamarind water, jaggery and salt.
Let it simmer.
After 5 mins add the rasam powder.
Add tadka with ghee, mustard seeds, jeera, curry leaves, hing.
Garnish with coriander.
Serve with hot rice and ghee and papad.

title: Sabudana Kheer
method: In a big vessel add milk, to it add the sugar, elaichi powder and the soaked sabudana.
Add the condensed milk if you plan on adding it.
Keep stirring until it’s cooked on low flame until it swells up and is done.
Garnish with saffron, almonds and cashews.
It has a thick and porridge-like consistency.
Serve hot or cold.

title: Creamy Cold Pasta Salad
method: In a bowl toss in the veggies, add the sauce, salt, pepper, and a little oregano or parsley.
Toss in the pasta and mix well.
Garnish with cheese and can leave it in the refrigerator and serve cold.

title: Pink Pasta
method: In a pan, add olive oil, chopped onions, and garlic (optional).
Fry.
Add the tomato sauce and white sauce and salt and pepper.
Let it boil a bit.
You can add plain tomatoes instead of the sauce and fry well.
In the end add basil and toss in the pasta.
Serve with the cheese.

title: Pesto Pasta
method: In a pan add the olive oil, to it add chopped onions, garlic, and fry.
Add the pesto sauce and wait until the oil separates.
Add pepper, salt, and chili flakes if you desire.
Add the veggies and fry a bit.
Add the pasta and toss it well.
Serve with cheese.
(Optional: add marinara as well for a blended sauce).

title: White Pasta

ingredients.item: Bechamel sauce (maida, butter, warm milk, cheddar cheese)
ingredients.quantity: 1/4 cup maida, 1/4 block butter, 1 cup milk

method: In a pan add butter, to it add onions and garlic.
Fry.
Add the veggies and fry.
Add the white sauce (if too thick, add a little milk).
Mix well.
Add the salt and pepper and toss in the pasta at the end.
Garnish with cheese.
Serve hot.

title: Pasta Primavera

ingredients.item: Veggies (asparagus, broccoli, cherry tomatoes, onion, garlic, carrots, peas, spinach)

method: In a pan add olive oil or butter.
To it add the chopped onion and garlic.
Fry.
Add veggies of your choice and fry them well but make sure you retain the crunch.
Add the cherry tomatoes at the very end.
Add salt, pepper, and chilli flakes (optional).
Add boiled pasta and toss.
Garnish with cheese and serve hot.

title: Indian Style Pasta

ingredients.item: Red chilli powder, chilli flakes, black pepper powder, salt

method: In a pan add olive oil and onions.
Fry until golden.
Add ginger garlic paste and fry until the raw smell goes.
Add tomatoes and red chilli powder and fry.
Add salt, pepper, and chilli flakes.
Mix in the pasta and add cheese at the very end.
Serve with tomato ketchup.

title: Marinara Sauce Recipe
method: Puree the tomatoes.
In a pan add olive oil, to it add chopped onions, garlic.
Fry well.
Add the bay leaf, chopped basil and fry.
Add the tomato puree, nutmeg powder, sugar, pepper, salt and water.
Let it thicken.
Discard the bay leaf before use.

title: Aglio E Olio (Olive Oil Pasta)
method: In a pan add olive oil, has to be very hot (be careful not to burn yourself), add chopped garlic, parsley, red pepper flakes and fry a bit.
Add salt and 2-3 spoons of pasta water to get the sauce consistency.
Now toss in the pasta and mix well.
Serve hot.

title: Mangalore Southekayi / Dosakayi And Tondekayi / Tindora Sambhar
method: In an open pot boil the southekayi and tondekayi.
Once 3/4 boiled, add tamarind, salt and a little jaggery to it.
Grind the roasted and cooled masala (dhania, jeera, methi, coconut).
Add it to the veggies.
Add the mashed toor dal and let it boil well.
Adjust the water and seasonings.
Add tadka with ghee, mustard seeds, jeera, curry leaves and hing.
Serve with hot rice and jackfruit papad.

title: Corn Raitha
method: Boil corn and separate the kernels.
Beat the curd a little, add red chilli powder, salt, sugar and mix.
Add it to the corn kernels.
Add chopped green chillies and coriander.

title: Gongura Pachadi / Tokku
method: In a pan in a little oil fry methi seeds, red chillies.
Allow it to cool.
Powder it with hing in a mixer.
Now in the same pan fry the dried leaves in oil until it wilts and you see no moisture in the leaves.
Let it cool down.
Grind it with the methi and chillies mixture along with salt.
Once cool, add this to some oil in a pan and fry well until the oil separates.
Add tadka made with oil, mustard seeds, red chillies, curry leaves, hing.
Mix well and once cool, store it in an airtight jar in the refrigerator.

title: Moong Sprout Salad

ingredients.item: Fruits (Blueberries, oranges, green apple, pomegranate seeds)

method: In a bowl mix all the veggies, moong sprouts, and fruits.
Add lemon, pepper, salt and toss well.
Garnish with coriander.

title: Kutchi Dabeli
method: Boil potato.
In a pan add butter, jeera, onion (optional), fry peanuts and deskin them, boiled potato, dabeli masala and mix.
Once cool garnish with pomegranate seeds and coriander.
While serving toast the pav and spread sweet chutney on one side and green chutney on the other side.
Make a small ball/ Patty with the dabeli mixture.
Garnish with sweet chutney, green chutney, onion, coriander and sev before serving.

title: Nombu Kanji
method: In a blender, powder rice and dal to a coarse grainy mixture.
In a pressure cooker add ghee, add the whole spices, onions, slit green chilli, ginger garlic paste.
Fry well.
To it add the tomato, dry powders, fry well.
Add finely chopped diced veggies, salt.
Now add the powdered rice, dal mixture & coconut milk if you wish.
To it add water, pudina and coriander, lemon juice and adjust the seasonings.
Close the pressure cooker and cook for 3-4 whistles.
Once the pressure releases add more water if needed and mash it with a masher so the veggies are mushy.
Serve it hot.

title: Sindhi Khichdi
method: In the pressure cooker add the washed rice and dals, tomato.
To it add tadka made with ghee, mustard seeds, little jeera, hing, curry leaves, sambhar powder, garlic, red chili powder, salt, haldi.
Cover the Cooker and cook for 3-4 whistles with a little extra water.
Once the steam releases mash the khichdi well with a spoon and serve it hot with ghee and pickle.

title: MTR Sagu / Bombay Sagu
method: In a pan add oil and make tadka.
To it add onions, ginger and fry well.
Add tomatoes cubed to it and cook it until well done.
Add red chilli powder, salt, Haldi, pinch of garam masala.
Fry.
Add the potatoes and water and let it boil.
Garnish with coriander.

title: Mango Pie
method: Boil water, dissolve gelatin in the water, let it cool.
In a blender, blend mango puree, cream cheese, sugar, milk, and the gelatin.
Pour it into the graham cracker pie crust.
Garnish with fruits and nuts of your choice.
Allow it to set in the refrigerator for about 6 hours.
You can serve it plain or with whipped cream.

title: Okra(Bhindi Raita)
method: In a pan add oil, to it add the chopped bhindi and fry well until almost black and crisp.
Once cool, add it to the curd.
Grind coconut, green chillies, coriander and mustard seeds into a smooth consistency.
Add salt and the ground masala to the curd and mix well.
Make a tadka with mustard seeds, curry leaves and hing.
Add salt and serve.

title: Quinoa Puliyogre
method: Boil quinoa with 2.5 cups of water (preferably in a rice cooker).
Make a tadka: add oil, mustard seeds, peanuts, hing, curry leaves, red chillies, chana and urad dal and fry well.
Add the puliyogre paste or gojju and salt.
Once cool mix the quinoa.
Garnish with dry coconut if desired.
Serve with raita or plain yogurt.

title: Spinach,Corn And 3 Cheese Lasagne.
method: Marinara sauce: In olive oil add garlic, bay leaf, basil, oregano, black pepper, salt, sugar and nutmeg.
Add tomato purée and water, boil well.
Spinach/Corn: In a pan add butter, blanch spinach, add corn and cook.
Bechamel sauce: Fry flour in butter, slowly add cream/half and half, stirring to avoid lumps.
Add spinach corn mixture and cheeses.
Lasagne: Cook sheets as per instructions, put in cold water with olive oil.
Assembly: Grease baking dish, add marinara sauce, layers of pasta, bechamel mixture, repeat.
Bake for 45 mins at 350°F.

title: Ganji / Conjee With Ganji Saru / Uppu Tili Saru

ingredients.item: red rice/ brown or white rice

method: Ganji: Cook rice in water on open flame with salt.
Drain excess water for saru.
Saru: Add coconut milk and buttermilk to Ganji water.
Add salt.
Make tadka with mustard, jeera, hing, curry leaves, green chillies and ginger.
Crush chillies and ginger once cool, add to saru.
Garnish with dhania leaves.

title: Paneer Tikki Pizza
method: Dough: Mix flour, yeast, salt, oil, water; knead and let rise.
Tikki: Mix dry powders, salt, hung curd; add paneer and veggies, lemon juice, ajwain, kasoori methi.
Refrigerate 2 hrs.
Sauce: Boil tomato puree with salt, oregano, basil, sugar, pepper until thick.
Assembly: Fry paneer mixture slightly.
Roll dough, brush with oil, poke holes.
Spread sauce, add paneer mixture and cheeses.
Bake at 475-500°F for 10-15 mins.

title: Brahmi Tambuli
method: In a pan add ghee, add pepper, jeera, Brahmi leaves and fry well.
Once cool, grind it in a mixer with coconut, curd and salt.
Add the tadka at the end.
Serve with rice.

title: Menthya Tambuli( Fenugreek Seeds).
method: Fry menthya until light brown.
Fry red chillies and jeera.
Grind all fried ingredients along with the coconut to a fine paste with the curd.
Add salt.
Make a tadka with ghee, mustard, jeera, curry leaves and hing.
Add to the paste.
Serve with hot rice.

title: Cucumber, Mint And Feta Salad.
method: In a bowl add olive oil, onions, black pepper powder, oregano, lemon juice, mint and give it a stir.
Add the chopped cucumber to it.
Mix.
Now add a little salt.
Crumble feta just before serving.

title: Hesaru Unde( Moong Dal Laddoo)
method: In a pan, add 1 spoon of ghee.
To it add the moong dal and fry until the raw smell disappears.
Allow it to cool and powder it along with the sugar in a mixer and sieve it to a fine powder.
Add the saffron, cardamom powder and the nuts to the sugar, dal mixture and mix well.
Warm the rest of the ghee and slowly add it to the mixture.
Make lemon sized balls.

title: Dalia (Broken Wheat Pulav)

ingredients.item: Veggies (Chopped Carrots, Green beans, Potatoes, Cauliflower florets, green peas)

method: Powder the whole spices (cloves, saunf, black pepper, and cinnamon) in a mortar.
Add 1/2 tsp ghee and 1/2 tsp oil in a pan.
To it add 1 bay leaf, 2 cardamom, and 1/2 tsp jeera.
Add chopped onions and green chilli paste; fry until the onion is golden brown.
Add 1/2 tsp of ginger garlic paste and fry well until the raw smell disappears.
Add fried cashews (optional).
Add tomatoes and the powdered whole garam masala; fry well.
Add veggies and salt, then fry for a bit.
Add washed broken wheat and fry for two minutes.
Add a pinch of garam masala powder, salt, and around 1.5 cups of water.
Garnish with mint and coriander.
Pressure cook for 2-3 whistles.
Once the pressure releases, fluff with a fork and serve hot with raita.
